root_text.an4.60:1.1: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an4.60:2.1: “Catūhi kho, gahapati, dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako gihisāmīcipaṭipadaṁ paṭipanno hoti yasopaṭilābhiniṁ saggasaṁvattanikaṁ.
root_text.an4.60:2.3: Idha, gahapati, ariyasāvako bhikkhusaṅghaṁ paccupaṭṭhito hoti cīvarena, bhikkhusaṅghaṁ paccupaṭṭhito hoti piṇḍapātena, bhikkhusaṅghaṁ paccupaṭṭhito hoti senāsanena, bhikkhusaṅghaṁ paccupaṭṭhito hoti gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārena.
root_text.an4.60:2.4: Imehi kho, gahapati, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako gihisāmīcipaṭipadaṁ paṭipanno hoti yasopaṭilābhiniṁ saggasaṁvattanikanti.

translation_text.an4.60:1.1: Then the householder Anāthapiṇḍika went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an4.60:2.1: “Householder, when a noble disciple does four things they are practicing appropriately for a layperson, which brings fame and leads to heaven.
translation_text.an4.60:2.3: It’s when a noble disciple serves the mendicant Saṅgha with robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an4.60:2.4: When a noble disciple does these four things they are practicing appropriately for a layperson, which brings fame and leads to heaven.
translation_text.an4.60:3.1: Those who are astute practice the way
translation_text.an4.60:3.3: They provide those who are ethical
translation_text.an4.60:4.5: They pass on to a place in heaven,

variant_text.an4.60:4.5: Saggañca kamatiṭṭhānaṁ → saggañca kamati ṭhānaṁ (pts1ed); saggañca sappatiṭṭhānaṁ (mr)

reference_text.an4.60:1.1: csp1ed15.376, csp2ed15.376, ms15A4_431, msdiv60, pts-vp-pli2.65, vri36.75
